nodes.title: Cultivating community
nodes.text: Building and nurturing relationships within the animal advocacy community to foster connection and collaboration

connections.evidence: Slack community doubled from 1,631 to 3,312 members in 2024, with 700+ weekly active users and 100,000+ messages exchanged (2024 Annual Report). Platform recognized as 'most active Slack community in broader EA Ecosystem' with 1,000+ monthly active users across 90+ channels.
connections.assumptions: Building authentic community relationships translates to increased platform value and engagement. Assumes community growth correlates with advocacy effectiveness and that digital community can substitute for some in-person relationship building.

connections.evidence: Community building activities directly feed resource development - events achieve 4.68/5 satisfaction scores with consistent maximum attendance. Newsletter content sourced from community discussions and needs assessment, achieving 50% open rate vs 21% industry average.
connections.assumptions: Community insights accurately reflect movement needs and that community-driven content development produces higher quality resources than expert-only approaches.

nodes.title: Centralizing key information
nodes.text: Gathering, organizing, and making accessible important resources and knowledge for the movement

connections.evidence: Newsletter reaches 3,160+ subscribers with 50% open rate and 10% click rate, significantly above industry standards. Automated Faunalytics research feeds provide access to 5,000+ study summaries. Resource hub received 7,500 page views in 2024 with fundraising database and conference listings.
connections.assumptions: Centralized information curation is more efficient than individual searching and that automated feeds maintain quality while scaling access. Quality of curated information remains high as volume increases.

nodes.title: Facilitating strategic connections
nodes.text: Actively identifying and enabling valuable relationships between advocates, organizations, and opportunities

connections.evidence: Team successfully facilitated 100+ strategic connections in 2024 plus 313 serendipitous matches through automated system. Documented outcomes include 29 paid job placements (13 permanent, 16 freelance/short-term) and 42 volunteer role placements.
connections.assumptions: Team's network knowledge and matching capabilities can scale with community growth. Strategic connection facilitation creates more value than organic networking alone.

nodes.title: Value of the Hive Slack and space for advocates promoted and improved
nodes.text: Activities to enhance and promote The Hive's digital platform and physical spaces as valuable resources for animal advocates

connections.evidence: 92% of community members report feeling stronger movement connection through platform (Community Survey 2024). Platform hosts 11 'highly engaged channels' with 100+ monthly viewers facilitating cross-organizational collaboration. ACE staff, Faunalytics researchers, and practitioners actively share insights.
connections.assumptions: Digital platform improvements translate to real-world collaboration increases and that Slack engagement metrics reflect genuine collaboration rather than just social activity.

nodes.title: Hive resources, events, and newsletter promoted and improved
nodes.text: Development and promotion of educational resources, community events, and regular communications to serve the movement

connections.evidence: Newsletter achieves 50% open rate reaching 3,160+ subscribers with regular features on funding opportunities, research updates, and movement news. Each edition costs $380 to produce ($9,120 annually) providing centralized information hub for global advocates.
connections.assumptions: Newsletter engagement rates reflect actual information consumption and that centralized information distribution improves movement-wide knowledge sharing more than decentralized approaches.

connections.evidence: Events program hosted 60+ events in 2024 with 4.68/5 satisfaction scores including skill-building workshops and fireside chats with movement leaders. Mental health partnership with CEVA resulted in 44 course sign-ups for advocate support.
connections.assumptions: Event participation translates to improved advocate capacity and that virtual/hybrid events can effectively build skills across geographic boundaries.

nodes.title: Promising connections identified and warm introductions made
nodes.text: Active networking and matchmaking to connect advocates with complementary skills, resources, or opportunities

connections.evidence: 100+ strategic connections facilitated directly by team in 2024, plus 313 automated matches through serendipitous matching system. Community surveys show advocates successfully finding co-founders, researchers connecting with implementers, and funders discovering promising projects.
connections.assumptions: Team's network knowledge enables identification of high-value connections and that warm introductions significantly increase connection success rates compared to cold outreach.

nodes.title: Better collaboration and coordination in animal advocacy
nodes.text: Organizations, advocates, and stakeholders work together more effectively, sharing resources and aligning strategies

connections.evidence: Platform facilitates information sharing between ACE staff, Faunalytics researchers, and practitioners that wouldn't otherwise circulate. 30+ regional channels and cause-specific channels enable coordinated information distribution across geographic and sectoral boundaries.
connections.assumptions: Enhanced collaboration leads to better information sharing and that cross-organizational information flows improve movement-wide decision making quality.

connections.evidence: 400+ help requests answered in Slack community, plus serendipitous matching system saves advocates 2-3 hours each by avoiding cold outreach (estimated 626-939 hours saved from 313 matches alone). Organizations report avoiding duplicated research efforts through platform.
connections.assumptions: Improved coordination reduces duplication more than it creates coordination overhead and that time savings are redirected to high-impact activities rather than consumed by increased coordination costs.

nodes.title: Advocates are better able to contribute
nodes.text: Individual advocates have improved skills, knowledge, and capacity to make meaningful contributions to the movement

connections.evidence: Mental health partnership with CEVA shows advocates accessing support (44 sign-ups). Platform enables mutual aid through help requests and resource sharing. Community surveys show high levels of peer support and knowledge exchange.
connections.assumptions: Increased advocate capacity translates to increased willingness and ability to support others and that skill/knowledge development creates positive feedback loops in community support.

connections.evidence: 11 new initiatives started through community connections in 2024. Community members report improved project quality through access to feedback, collaborators, and resources. Platform helps identify and avoid duplicate efforts through visibility.
connections.assumptions: Skilled advocates with better connections produce higher quality projects and that community feedback and collaboration improve project outcomes compared to solo efforts.

nodes.title: The right people meet at the right time
nodes.text: Strategic connections form between individuals with complementary skills, resources, and opportunities

connections.evidence: 313 serendipitous matches in 2024 saved approximately 626-939 hours of individual searching time. Strategic connections help advocates find resources, collaborators, and opportunities faster than traditional networking.
connections.assumptions: Right-time connections create efficiency gains and that strategic matching accelerates outcomes rather than just reallocating them within the movement.

connections.evidence: Strong evidence from documented outcomes where connections led to mutual support, collaboration opportunities, and resource sharing. Platform design specifically facilitates peer-to-peer assistance through help channels.
connections.assumptions: Strategic connections naturally evolve into ongoing support relationships and that platform design can effectively facilitate organic peer support networks.

connections.evidence: 29 paid job placements documented in 2024 (13 permanent, 16 freelance) plus 42 volunteer role placements. Success stories include advocates finding co-founders, researchers connecting with implementation opportunities, and funders discovering projects.
connections.assumptions: Strategic timing of talent-opportunity connections significantly increases placement success rates and that quality of connections matters more than quantity for talent placement.

nodes.title: A more and better-informed movement
nodes.text: The animal advocacy movement has access to higher-quality information and research, enabling better strategic decisions

connections.evidence: Newsletter achieves 50% open rate with 3,160+ subscribers accessing curated research from Faunalytics (5,000+ study summaries) and movement updates. Resource hub provides 7,500 page views annually of strategic guidance and evidence-based tools.
connections.assumptions: Evidence-based advocacy strategies significantly outperform intuition-based approaches and that access to high-quality information translates to better strategic decisions by movement actors. This is a critical assumption requiring testing through before/after outcome measurement of organizations using our resources.

nodes.title: Time and effort saved
nodes.text: Organizations and advocates avoid duplicated work and inefficient approaches, redirecting resources to high-impact activities

connections.evidence: Estimated 626-939 hours saved through serendipitous matching alone, plus 400+ help requests answered. Time savings enable advocates to focus on high-impact activities rather than searching, duplicating efforts, or reinventing solutions.
connections.assumptions: Time and effort savings are redirected to higher impact activities rather than consumed by coordination overhead or other low-impact work. Efficiency gains at individual level aggregate to movement-level impact acceleration.

nodes.title: Support offered and received
nodes.text: Movement actors provide and access mutual support, resources, and collaboration opportunities

connections.evidence: Mental health partnership (44 CEVA course sign-ups) and peer support systems help retain advocates longer in movement. Community surveys show 78% report increased motivation and likelihood of long-term involvement.
connections.assumptions: Supported advocates remain engaged longer and are more productive than unsupported advocates. Peer support systems reduce burnout rates and improve advocacy effectiveness over time.

nodes.title: Projects are started, quality is improved, and duplicates are avoided
nodes.text: New high-quality initiatives emerge while redundant efforts are minimized through better coordination

connections.evidence: 11 new initiatives started in 2024 through community connections, with quality improvements reported through feedback and collaboration. Duplication avoidance enables resources to flow to new projects rather than redundant efforts.
connections.assumptions: New high-quality projects create more impact than prevented duplicate projects would have created and that community feedback and collaboration improve project success rates and impact.

nodes.title: New better talent connected to opportunities
nodes.text: Skilled individuals discover and engage with high-impact opportunities in animal advocacy

connections.evidence: 29 paid job placements plus 42 volunteer roles placed in 2024. Strong anecdotal evidence of skilled advocates finding high-impact opportunities they wouldn't have discovered otherwise, particularly in neglected regions through Global Ambassador Program.
connections.assumptions: Better talent allocation to opportunities increases overall movement effectiveness and that strategic connections accelerate talent placement rather than just reallocating existing talent within the movement.

nodes.title: Less animal suffering and a faster end to factory farming
